plugins.name: castify
plugins.description: Make asciinema recordings scriptable: script every keystroke and pause instead of performing the session live, so casts are reproducible and automatable — and so you can even record interactive TUIs like fzf. Includes the tmux-driven harness and a web-embedding guide.

plugins.name: dev-kit
plugins.description: A Human + AI teaming development workflow. /dev-kit:ship orchestrates a change end to end (plan in a worktree, implement with tiered subagent delegation, simplify, refresh docs, review, PR); /dev-kit:review-pr runs a full review battery plus a context-chosen adversarial pass on your own or a teammate's PR; /dev-kit:generate-docs builds a self-contained docs site that works from file:// and GitHub Pages; /dev-kit:handle-task-tracking runs a repeatable workflow for tracking work as GitHub issues, which ship delegates to across the lifecycle; /dev-kit:open-work reads those open issues and returns the full ranked "pick up next" list, never truncated, with rationale for the standout picks, the select step between tracking and ship; /dev-kit:cleanup-locally prunes merged branches and worktrees and updates the default branch without clobbering local work, which ship runs at the start of a run and after a merge; /dev-kit:doctor checks installed plugin versions against the latest releases (flagging a stale cache running an old skill) and inventories the plugins and their skills; /dev-kit:dry-dock-overhaul performs an exhaustive, occasional, human-triggered whole-repo audit — every tracked file read and judged, plus a per-repo-discovered "10,000-foot" pass on communication and UX — orchestrating /dev-kit:review-pr, /dev-kit:generate-docs, and /dev-kit:pre-public-hardening alongside the net-new coverage.

plugins.name: worktree-guard
plugins.description: A PreToolUse safety hook that stops the classic git-worktree footgun: when your session is inside a .claude/worktrees/<name>/ worktree, it blocks a Write/Edit/MultiEdit whose path resolves into the parent checkout outside the worktree (the main copy you didn't mean to touch — usually a stray absolute path, but a relative path that escapes the worktree is caught too), while leaving the worktree's own files — and its own git dir, where tools like /dev-kit:ship keep run state — writable. Inert outside a worktree, and fail-open on any error.
